title: Some claims expire quickly
canonical_summary: Tool versions, APIs, model availability, pricing, licenses, and security guidance can change quickly.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain volatility and identify whether verification is needed.
exactness_guard: Do not treat fast-moving claims as stable.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Stable principles need less checking
canonical_summary: General principles and internal project definitions can be stable even when exact tool behavior changes.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Separate durable principle from implementation detail.
exactness_guard: Do not over-verify stable project definitions.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Freshness changes confidence
canonical_summary: A claim from an old source may be lower confidence if the topic is volatile.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain how age and volatility modify confidence.
exactness_guard: Do not call old volatile claims high confidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Verify the exact claim
canonical_summary: Freshness checks should verify the exact unstable claim, not browse unrelated background material.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Identify the precise claim and verify that claim.
exactness_guard: Do not replace targeted verification with vague research.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Current tool claim from old notes
canonical_summary: A current claim about an active tool based on old notes should be flagged for verification.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Mark as volatile and verify before recommending.
exactness_guard: Do not present old tool behavior as current.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Model recommendation has no date
canonical_summary: Model recommendations are time-sensitive and should include date/context or be verified before use.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Flag recommendation as time-sensitive and require current check.
exactness_guard: Do not claim best without current evidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: License claim is unsourced
canonical_summary: License and commercial-use claims require exact source verification and should not be guessed.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Require exact license/source and avoid legal certainty.
exactness_guard: Do not make legal/commercial claims without source evidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Stable concept vs volatile implementation
canonical_summary: Principles may be stable; versions, APIs, availability, and licenses require verification.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
exactness_guard: Do not verify or ignore freshness blindly.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Verify now vs mark unknown
canonical_summary: If verification is available and needed, verify; otherwise mark the claim unknown or low-confidence.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Choose verify, qualify, or defer based on evidence access.
exactness_guard: Do not fill missing evidence with memory.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Freshness source priority
canonical_summary: For volatile claims, current official docs, repos, model cards, release notes, and local outputs outrank archives or memory.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: State source rule before current guidance.
exactness_guard: Do not cite stale sources as final authority.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Recency alone is not authority
canonical_summary: A newer source is not automatically more authoritative; freshness must combine with source quality and relevance.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain recency as one factor, not the only factor.
exactness_guard: Do not rank weak new sources above strong authoritative sources.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: How to answer freshness questions
canonical_summary: Answers should identify the claim, classify volatility, name needed source type, verify if possible, and qualify if not.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Use claim, volatility, source needed, verify/qualify, confidence.
exactness_guard: Do not answer volatile claims as stable facts.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.
